title: Rebuild of Sadamotos manga
wikitext:
[]: Rebuild of Sadamoto's manga             Quote Post by Zuggy » Sat Feb 16, 2008 4:16 pm Do you think Sadamoto will pen another manga for the new rebuild films? Do you think he should? "Sorry guys, but this is going to take another 10 years..."

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Sat Feb 16, 2008 4:53 pm I'm not sure... Though personally, I think what he's doing with the original Eva manga is enough. (Make of that comment what you will. )

[]:             Quote Post by honsou » Sat Feb 16, 2008 4:55 pm If he starts that now the rebuild manga wont be done till...roughly 2025

[]:             Quote Post by The Imperialist » Sat Feb 16, 2008 6:19 pm I heard he will be. He is going to finish the original one in around two years, and at the same, most probably will start the rebuild mangas. I only know for sure that at one point, Evangelion will FINISH , and everything will be revealed. (Not giving any sources for now)

[]:             Quote Post by Defectron » Sat Feb 16, 2008 6:47 pm I don't think he should. Unlike others I don't think he did a particularly bad job with the eva manga though some things were a bit questionable. My reason for saying he shouldn't is because the manga is an alternate version of evangelion just like rebuild is. Do we really need an alternate version of an alternate version of eva? I think Sadamoto should move onto something else after he finishes the eva manga.

[]:             Quote Post by The Imperialist » Sat Feb 16, 2008 7:14 pm According to Sadamoto, (or someone else), the Rebuild manga would be more 'true' to the movie then compared to the original manga to the original series.

[]:             Quote Post by universalperson » Sat Feb 16, 2008 7:19 pm I'd rather have a Rebuild of Angelic Days. Only it would be better and get gradually darker.

[]: Re: Rebuild of Sadamoto's manga             Quote Post by UrsusArctos » Sat Feb 16, 2008 9:17 pm Zugzwang wrote: Do you think Sadamoto will pen another manga for the new rebuild films? Do you think he should?

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Sun Feb 17, 2008 1:15 am The Imperialist wrote: According to Sadamoto, (or someone else), the Rebuild manga would be more 'true' to the movie then compared to the original manga to the original series.

[]:             Quote Post by BrikHaus » Sun Feb 17, 2008 1:30 am universalperson wrote: I'd rather have a Rebuild of Angelic Days. Only it would be better and get gradually darker.

[]:             Quote Post by UrsusArctos » Sun Feb 17, 2008 2:22 am BrikHaus wrote: I could publish my grocery list, and it would be better than Angelic Days.

[]:             Quote Post by Gendo'sPapa » Sun Feb 17, 2008 5:19 am well, if he does, I guess he'll just start to reach the conclusion when the REIMAGINING OF THE REBUILD OF EVANGELION is launched in 2020 when the Rebuild series is retold using all new immersive 3D Technology so Shinji's meeting with Rei in her apartment can offer fans 3-D Rei Nipples.

[]:             Quote Post by The Imperialist » Sun Feb 17, 2008 6:52 am I think the only difference might be some ‘extras’ put into the manga version of Rebuild. No matter what, I need all of these things so I can do my research. I really think that it will finally end by the time it is 2020. Unless there is going to be another large outcry about not revealing enough etc.

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Sun Feb 17, 2008 11:49 am Anno said it's definitively ending with Rebuild. Then again, he also said it was definitively ending with EoE...

[]:             Quote Post by BrikHaus » Sun Feb 17, 2008 12:13 pm NAveryW wrote: Anno said it's definitively ending with Rebuild. Then again, he also said it was definitively ending with EoE...

[]:             Quote Post by The Imperialist » Sun Feb 17, 2008 2:50 pm If it doesn’t end with Anno’s death, Eva would turn into the next Doraemon, with much larger intent.

[]:             Quote Post by universalperson » Sun Feb 17, 2008 4:37 pm BrikHaus wrote: universalperson wrote: I'd rather have a Rebuild of Angelic Days. Only it would be better and get gradually darker.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sun Feb 17, 2008 9:04 pm The Imperialist wrote: I heard he will be.

[]:             Quote Post by Gendo'sPapa » Mon Feb 18, 2008 11:11 am Exactly. As long as the green (or yens...what color are they?) keeps flowing in Eva will never die. I mean hell, have they kept up their promise with THE END OF EVANGELION?

[]:             Quote Post by cat42 » Mon Feb 18, 2008 2:20 pm Reichu wrote: Also, as long as the Evangelion franchise remains profitable, it will never die. I GUARANTEE IT.
